events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to identify the component or condition shown, and name the visible defect cues. The scenario: Structural welding, observed field condition: visible undercut/porosity/incomplete fusion. Work stage: rough-complete. Reference: AWS D1.1. We need to output JSON with decision, risk, etc.

The condition is defective: undercut, porosity, incomplete fusion are welding defects. According to AWS D1.1, these are unacceptable. So decision should be "fail". Risk: likely high or critical, depending on severity. Since it's structural welding, defects can compromise integrity, so risk "high" or "critical". I'll go with "high" as it's not yet in-service, but still structural. Work stage: "rough-complete". Component conditions: "installed-defective" fits. Percent complete: maybe 80%? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out, so maybe 80-90%. I'll put 85. Value: no numeric reading, so 0. Sound source: not asked, so empty string. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate: not applicable, 0. Order: empty list. Workable: maybe none, since it's defective, but could be rework activities. I'll leave empty. Findings: list of defect cues: "Undercut", "Porosity", "Incomplete fusion". Actions: "Remove and replace defective weld", "Re-weld per AWS D1.1", "Re-inspect". Rationale: "Visible welding defects (undercut, porosity, incomplete fusion) violate AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria for structural welds." References: ["AWS D1.1"]. 

So JSON output.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Undercut",
    "Porosity",
    "Incomplete fusion"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and replace defective weld",
    "Re-weld in accordance with AWS D1.1",
    "Perform visual and NDT inspection after repair"
  ],
  "rationale": "Visible welding defects (undercut, porosity, incomplete fusion) violate AWS D1.1 acceptance criteria for structural welds, compromising joint integrity.",
  "references": ["AWS D1.1"]
}
